translations.source: Records of the Three Kingdoms [三國志]
translations.source_text: 《後漢書》曰：尚書幘收，方三寸，名曰納言，示以忠正，顯近職也。
translations.translation:
Wang Fan (Yongyuan)
王蕃 (永元)
Lived ADca.227–266
aSanguozhiBiographyRecords of the Three Kingdoms [三國志]
byZL181
onFebruary 2nd, 2024

Wang Fan, styledYongyuan, was fromLujiang[廬江].
He had extensive knowledge and was well versed. He also understood esoteric numerology.1He first servedGentleman of the Secretariat, but left office. AfterSun Xiuascended the throne, he,He Shao,Xue Ying, andYu Siall served asCavalier Regular Palace Attendants, and they all were additionally conferred to beCommandants of Escorting Cavalry. Contemporary opinions regarded them as incorrupt. He was sent as an envoy toShuand the people ofShupraised him. He returned to serve asArmy SupervisorofXiakou[夏口監軍].•••
Here, “esoteric numerology” [術藝] refers to calculations being applied to astronomy, calendar calculations, divination, and weather.Wang Fanwas a notable astronomer and mathematician.↩
During the early reign ofSun Hao, he again entered asRegular Palace Attendant, sharing the same rank asWan Yu.[Wan] Yuwas friendly with[Sun] Hao, but he was a rustic fellow who was narrow-minded and sensitive, and he alleged that[Wang] Fanbelittled him. In addition, theDeputy of the Central Depository,Chen Sheng, was a favored minister of[Sun] Hao, and he slandered[Wang] Fanon numerous occasions.[Wang] Fanwas noble and candid in demeanor and spirit, he would not have a complaisant expression or be submissive to orders. Sometimes, he would go against [imperial] wishes, receiving censures after censures.
In the second year of theera [266],Ding Zhongreturned from his diplomatic mission toJin.[Sun] Haocalled a major meeting of the officials, but[Wang] Fanwas deeply intoxicated that he stumbled and fell.[Sun] Haobecame suspicious and displeased. He had[Wang] Fancarried outside, who soon requested to return. The wine had not detoxicated.[Wang] Fanby nature was dignified and strict, and his conduct was to his comfort.[Sun] Haowas furious and called his attendants at the halls to behead him. TheGeneral of the Guards,Teng Mu, and theGeneral who Conquers the West,Liu Ping, requested mercy, but they did notsucceed.A
TheBiographies of the Region South of the Riverstates:[Sun] Haoused the words of history-oriented sorcerers, who said that the palace atJianye[建業] was not beneficial and that he should move west toWuchang[武昌]. Thus, he had intentions of moving the capital, but feared that his officials would not go along with him, so he assembled a large meeting and gave gifts to generals and officials. He asked[Wang] Fan, “‘In archery, going through the leather is not important, for strength is never equal.’ What does it mean?”2[Wang] Fanthought deeply about it, but could not reply, so [Sun Hao] beheaded[Wang] Fanin the palace hall. He then left and went up a mountain, then had his intimate generals trample the head of[Wang] Fansuch that they were like tigers and wolves competing in gnawing it. The head was entirely destroyed and ruined. He wanted to show off his might through this in order to cause the masses to never even dare offend him.
This is different from the basebiography.•••
Archery was regarded as a skill of gentlemanly scholars. This quote attributed toKong Qiulearning over brute strength.Sun Haoseems to be askingWang FanifWang Fanregards him as a scholarly emperor, or at least competent enough to use a bow.↩
TheRecords of Wustates: Each time[Sun] Haowas at a meeting, he became intoxicated. He would then order his attending ministers to satirize and tease theExcellenciesorMinisters, laughing and having fun.Wan Yuserved as the LeftAssisting Chancellor, and[Wang] Fanmocked[Wan] Yuand said, “Fish reside underwater in the abyss, but when they leave the water, they salivate [then die]. Why? Because living things have their own innate nature: they cannot presumptuously be where they are not assigned to be.[Wan] Yu, you came from [lowly] valleys, but you have the body of a sheep with the skin of a tiger.3Still, you received glorious favor and you have surpassed the position of the Three [Excellencies] and Nine [Ministers]. Even dogs and horses can recognize their caretaker. What will you do to repay such generous gifts?”[Wan] Yusaid, “The courts ofTangandYuwere devoid of the capability to make erroneous recommendations; and regarding the household of Zaofu [造父],4they had no essence of being like slow steeds. You,[Wang] Fan, have defamed the enlightened appointments [of the sovereign] and ridiculed [essential ministers who are as] trunks. But how can this harm the sun or moon?5It only shows that you do not know how to [properly] estimate [your ownabilities]!”•••
Wang Fansays thatWan Yuappears powerful, but that he is really weak like a sheep.↩
Zaofu was a famous charioteer who was said to cover thousands of miles a day.↩
The sun and the moon here refer to the emperor and his ministers, who are the superiors toWang Fan.↩
Your minister, [Pei] Songzhi, would inspect this. The base biography states that whenDing Zhongreturned from his diplomatic mission toJin,[Sun] Haoheld a major meeting , and in the meeting, he killed[Wang] Fan. I examined that when[Ding] Zhongreturned from the north in the spring of the year,[Wan] Yuwas still notAssisting Chancellorat that time. It was only in the fall that he became chancellor. What theRecords of Wustates is contradictory and different.
Assisting ChancellorLu Kaipetitioned:
“Regular AttendantWang Fanwas one who was moderate as the color yellow and comprehended reason. He understood Heaven and he understood concepts. Within the administration, he was loyal and outspoken. Thus, he was a critical fence of theAltars of State—the [Guan] Longfeng [關龍逢] ofGreatWu!6Of old, he served theReveredSovereignas a close attendant wearing a Nayan hat.7TheReveredSovereignrespected and admired him, praising him for his different opinions. Yet,Your Majestydetested his bitter speech and hated his honest replies, so you hung his head in the palace hall and exposed his corpse in the city center. The country feels pained, and those with foresightmourn!”•••
Guan Longfeng was an outspoken minister who lived during theXia dynasty. He criticized the last king for his wanton and drunken behavior. In response, he was killed in a brutal fashion.↩
TheBook of Later Hanstates: The headwear of the Secretariat is threecunin area. It is named the Nayan hat, and it shows loyalty and righteousness. It signifies closeness to his duty. [《後漢書》曰：尚書幘收，方三寸，名曰納言，示以忠正，顯近職也。] This is likely a metaphor to show thatWang Fanwas dedicated and in attendance.↩
The grief for[Wang] Fanwas as such.[Wang] Fanwas thirty-nine years old when he died.[Sun] Haoexiled the family of[Wang] FantoGuang province[廣州]. His two younger brothers,[Wang] Zhuand[Wang] Yan, were both virtuous and talented. During the rebellion ofGuo Ma, they did not serve[Guo] Ma, so they were killed.
